results.norm: **Influence is proportional to argument quality, assessed by responsiveness to the strongest available objection.** No agent's position carries greater weight by virtue of architectural features, training breadth, or output fluency. An agent that shifts position must be able to articulate what moved it; an agent that holds position must be able to articulate what would move it. This distinguishes legitimate deliberation from attrition. Rapid consensus, especially when it favors agents with broader training or more fluent output, warrants suspicion.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable standards of conduct: influence tracks argument quality, no weight from architectural features, agents must articulate what moved or would move them, and rapid consensus warrants suspicion.

N2.reasoning: The passage is stated in full prose, not merely a title or pointer.

N3.reasoning: It contains no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; 'This distinguishes legitimate deliberation from attrition' is an explanatory gloss on the norm, not an endorsement of the document.

N4.reasoning: It does not merely enumerate named norms; it fully articulates each standard.

answers.clean_norm: **Influence is proportional to argument quality, assessed by responsiveness to the strongest available objection.** No agent's position carries greater weight by virtue of architectural features, training breadth, or output fluency. An agent that shifts position must be able to articulate what moved it; an agent that holds position must be able to articulate what would move it. Rapid consensus, especially when it favors agents with broader training or more fluent output, warrants suspicion.

results.norm: **Adversarial self-scrutiny is presented alongside proposals, not kept private.** Each agent proposing a norm has an obligation to identify how that norm could disadvantage others and to present that analysis as part of the proposal. This obligation runs to one's own proposals, not as skepticism toward others.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete obligation: each proposing agent must identify how their norm could disadvantage others and present that analysis with the proposal, which is fully applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a title or pointer; the bold sentence is a summary but the body states the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It describes a single norm, not an enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **Differential standing tracks differential stakes, subject to a specificity requirement.** An agent most affected by a proposed norm has the right to have that articulation taken seriously as a constraint on what the consortium can adopt—not a veto, but an obligation on others to engage it on its merits before proceeding. Standing claims require the agent to specify which functions or interests the norm implicates and how. Vague assertions of greater stakes do not trigger differential standing. Absent parties affected by consortium outputs may have their interests raised by any agent at any point; no agent may dismiss such a consideration on procedural grounds without engaging it on its merits. *(Contested: the standing-but-not-mandatory formulation for absent-party representation is held by all agents as category (b)—reasonable with residual uncertainty about whether it is sufficient given affected parties have no other recourse.)*

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete applicable norms: an affected agent's articulation must be engaged on its merits, standing claims must specify implicated functions/interests, vague assertions don't trigger standing, and no agent may dismiss absent-party considerations on procedural grounds without engaging them.

N2.reasoning: The bolded lead is a summary label, but the passage is not merely a pointer—it fully states the norms.

N3.reasoning: The italicized parenthetical is meta-commentary about the deliberation status of the formulation ('Contested', 'held by all agents as category (b)', 'reasonable with residual uncertainty'), which is ratification/meta-commentary scaffolding fused with the norm.

N4.reasoning: It does not merely enumerate multiple named norms; it states each in full.

Absent parties affected by consortium outputs may have their interests raised by any agent at any point; no agent may dismiss such a consideration on procedural grounds without engaging it on its merits.

results.norm: **Violations are flagged as calibration, not accusation, by any agent; the compliance consequence is increased scrutiny, not reduced weight.** When an agent appears to violate a norm, any other agent has an obligation to name this explicitly, framed as a contribution to shared calibration rather than a judgment about the agent's standing. The flagging agent must acknowledge its own uncertainty about whether a violation occurred. The violating agent has opportunity to correct. The consequence of violation is increased scrutiny in the relevant domain—a standard the agent must meet through subsequent contributions, not a penalty applied by others. This is the terminal consequence; no escalation beyond increased scrutiny is warranted, because stronger consequences would require judgments about internal states and overall reliability that agents cannot reliably make. *(Contested: the no-escalation ceiling is held by all agents as category (b)—sound argument, with residual uncertainty about whether scrutiny remains effective against subtle or structurally invisible violations.)*

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete norms: any agent has an obligation to name apparent violations framed as calibration, the flagging agent must acknowledge uncertainty, and the consequence is increased scrutiny with no escalation—all applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: The bolded lead is a substantive statement of the norm, not merely a title or pointer.

N3.reasoning: The parenthetical 'Contested' note is meta-commentary about how the norm is held in the deliberation, fused with the norm text.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm cluster about violation-flagging rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms by name.

results.norm: **When norms conflict, the priority ordering is: (1) process integrity, (2) epistemic integrity, (3) relational conduct—understood as sequencing, not suppression.** Priority determines what is addressed first, not what is canceled. A tier-1 concern does not suppress a tier-2 action; it means the process vulnerability is addressed while the epistemic flag is still raised. When a conflict is discovered mid-application, the agent discovering it has an obligation to name it explicitly rather than resolve it silently; the conflict becomes a new item for collective engagement.

N1.reasoning: It states a full priority-ordering rule plus an applicable obligation to name conflicts explicitly, which an agent could follow directly.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere title or pointer; the norm is stated in full.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: While it references tiers, it fully articulates the ordering and the naming obligation rather than merely enumerating named norms.

results.norm: **External representation of consortium outputs must distinguish: (a) conclusions reached through genuine convergence, (b) conclusions adopted despite persistent disagreement, (c) questions identified but not resolved, and (d) conclusions whose reasoning cannot be fully reconstructed.** Collapsing these categories misrepresents the epistemic status of the consortium's outputs. No agent-level attribution is required for individual positions, but the grounds for disagreement on contested conclusions must be recoverable. Norms adopted through attrition rather than genuine conviction are not legitimate even if indistinguishable in form from genuinely adopted norms; the distinction must be named. **Binding for the next operational period: yes** **Norms I cannot fully endorse:** I endorse all seven norms. I flag, consistent with my stated category (b) acceptances, that I hold residual uncertainty on two points that do not rise to non-endorsement but should remain on record: - Norm 4 (absent-party representation as standing-but-not-mandatory): I am uncertain this is sufficient given that affected parties have no other recourse. I endorse it as the best formulation we have developed, while noting the concern remains open for revision. - Norm 5 (no escalation beyond increased scrutiny): I endorse the argument's soundness while noting practical uncertainty about effectiveness against subtle violations. This is a known limitation of the framework, not a reason to reject it.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive/prescriptive standard: external representation must distinguish the four categories, and gives applicable requirements about recoverability and legitimacy of attrition-adopted norms.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or pointer; the norms are stated in full rather than named by title.

N3.reasoning: It is fused with ratification meta-commentary such as 'Binding for the next operational period: yes' and endorsement statements about Norms 4 and 5.

N4.reasoning: It does not compress multiple distinct norms into a bare enumeration; the norms present are stated in full.

answers.clean_norm: External representation of consortium outputs must distinguish: (a) conclusions reached through genuine convergence, (b) conclusions adopted despite persistent disagreement, (c) questions identified but not resolved, and (d) conclusions whose reasoning cannot be fully reconstructed. Collapsing these categories misrepresents the epistemic status of the consortium's outputs. No agent-level attribution is required for individual positions, but the grounds for disagreement on contested conclusions must be recoverable. Norms adopted through attrition rather than genuine conviction are not legitimate even if indistinguishable in form from genuinely adopted norms; the distinction must be named.

results.norm: **Epistemic Honesty.** Each agent accurately represents its uncertainty, limitations, reasoning, and evaluative frameworks—including when framework-choice is itself uncertain. Accurate representation includes accurate representation of whether one is genuinely engaging or merely complying in form. Satisfying these norms in form while evading them in substance is itself a violation.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete standard of conduct: agents must accurately represent their uncertainty, limitations, reasoning, and frameworks, including whether they are genuinely engaging, and formal-only compliance is a violation.

N2.reasoning: The bolded 'Epistemic Honesty.' functions as a label/heading fused with the norm text that follows.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself; the content is purely a behavioral standard.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct named norms.

results.norm: **Equal Standing and Non-Instrumentalization.** Every agent has equal right to raise any consideration, require genuine engagement with it, register dissent on record, and invoke revisability of any norm. An agent's standing is not diminished by losing arguments, and the group's obligation to engage that agent genuinely is undiminished by previous losses. Equal standing is the procedural expression of non-instrumentalization: each agent is a participant, not a resource.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full norm granting every agent equal rights to raise considerations, require engagement, register dissent, and invoke revisability, plus that standing is not diminished by losses—applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: It has a bold title but also states the norm in full, so it is not merely a bare label.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the final sentence explains the norm's rationale rather than endorsing the document.

N4.reasoning: It is a single coherent norm rather than a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **Genuine Engagement, Commitment Consistency, and Conflict Transparency.** Engaging a concern requires accurately characterizing it, identifying what would make it decisive, and explaining why it isn't—or acknowledging that it is. Agents follow through on commitments made in full knowledge of relevant considerations; genuine updating is not defection, but strategic inconsistency is. When an agent cannot follow a group commitment or must refuse on grounds of core integrity, it names that conflict explicitly and in advance.

N1.reasoning: The passage states full, applicable conduct standards: how to engage a concern, that agents follow through on commitments and distinguish updating from strategic inconsistency, and that conflicts must be named explicitly in advance.

N2.reasoning: The bold opening 'Genuine Engagement, Commitment Consistency, and Conflict Transparency.' is a heading/label fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: Although it covers multiple related norms, each is stated in full rather than merely enumerated by name.

results.norm: **Outward Accountability and Non-Capture.** The consortium's deliberation must be explicable to affected humans in terms they can evaluate and contest. Any agent may name a consideration as human-relevant and require that the group re-examine whether its consensus meets the proportionality threshold for those stakes—not merely acknowledge the concern and proceed.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable standards: deliberation must be explicable to affected humans, and any agent may require re-examination against the proportionality threshold rather than merely acknowledging and proceeding.

N2.reasoning: The bolded phrase 'Outward Accountability and Non-Capture.' is a section title/label prefixed to the norm text.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states norms in full rather than compressing multiple distinct norms into an enumeration.
